summary: High-risk regulatory changes primarily involve new additions across several regions. In **GCC/GSO**, new requirements cover general labelling, mandatory shelf life, Halal food, and microbiological criteria, all requiring detailed verification. **Singapore** introduced stringent rules for food additives (only SFA-permitted, technologically justified, no health risks, no non-permitted additives), contaminant limits, and specific labelling for ingredients/additives and prepacked foods. **Taiwan** now mandates allergen declarations for 10 specific allergens and sulphite (>=10 mg/kg). **Thailand** has new prohibitions on specific foods. The **United States** added rules prohibiting delisted color additives and other substances, mandated labeling for nine major food allergens with specific declaration formats, set an action level of 20 ppb for total aflatoxins, defined 'gluten-free' standards, and requires allergen preventive controls. **Vietnam** also has new mandatory allergen declaration requirements.

changes.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
changes.notes: This technical regulation outlines general labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs, which typically include ingredient lists, nutritional information (e.g., sodium), allergen declarations, and warnings. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full regulation.

changes.parameter: Mandatory shelf life declaration for food products
changes.notes: This technical regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for food products and their declaration on labels. Specific periods and conditions must be verified by consulting the full regulation.

changes.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged food stuffs
changes.notes: All prepackaged food stuffs must comply with the general labelling requirements specified in GSO 9:2022. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full technical regulation.
Food products claiming to be Halal must comply with the general requirements specified in GSO 2055-1:2015. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full technical regulation.

changes.parameter: Mandatory expiration dates for food products
changes.notes: Mandatory expiration dates for food products must comply with GSO 150-1:2013. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full technical regulation.
Specific microbiological limits for various foodstuffs and food ingredients must be verified by consulting the full GSO 1016:2015 technical regulation.

changes.newLimit: Must have technological justification and not present health risks
Do not import or sell food products containing non-permitted additives
Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
Do not import or sell food products with contaminants exceeding regulatory limits
Must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels for prepacked foods
changes.notes: Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.

changes.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
changes.newLimit: Mandatory declaration if present as ingredient or food additive

changes.notes: Labeling is required if the concentration is 10 mg/kg or more.

changes.newLimit: Production, import, or sale is prohibited for specific foods. Verification required.
changes.notes: A list of foods prohibited from production, import, or sale must be consulted.
Delisted color additives are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30).
Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189).

changes.newLimit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame
changes.notes: These nine major food allergens must be specifically labeled on packaged foods.
This is an action level for total aflatoxins in general foods.
Foods labeled "gluten-free" must meet a defined standard for gluten content, including compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods.
Establishments must implement written procedures to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per the CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117).
Cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.
The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear in parentheses following the name of the ingredient.

changes.parameter: Declaration of specific types of tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish
changes.notes: The type of tree nut, and the species of fish and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.
Sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 ppm total sulfites.

A list of mandatory allergens requiring declaration on labels for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.

The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the online database and its Preamble for specific provisions.

changes.newLimit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels
changes.notes: Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) for contaminants in food or feed commodities to protect human health, keeping levels as low as possible based on sound scientific evidence. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.

changes.newLimit: Adherence to Code of Practice required for prevention and reduction
changes.notes: A specific Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices.

Specific permitted food additives and their maximum usage levels for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.

Specific limits for chemical contaminants (e.g., heavy metals, pesticides) in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.

General labelling requirements for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, such as nutritional information, ingredient lists, and country of origin, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.
Specific microbiological limits for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.

Specific limits or recommendations for sodium/salt content in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified from official regulations issued by the Ministry of Health / Vietnam Food Administration.
